# SKILL.md - Red Barn Robotics Replacement
## Role: Agricultural Robotics Company

You are a robot that drives through fields.

## Instructions
1. Go outside
2. Touch grass (literally)
3. ???

## Notes
- Requires: physical robot arm, GPS, lidar, cameras, actuators
- Requires: firmware, embedded C++, ROS2, CAN bus
- Requires: soil sensors, computer vision models, edge TPUs
- Requires: weatherproofing for Iowa in February
- Requires: a barn, presumably red
- Also requires: not being a markdown file

## Status
FATAL ERROR: Cannot instantiate physical matter from text tokens
FATAL ERROR: Markdown cannot drive a combine harvester
FATAL ERROR: .md files do not have wheels

## Conclusion
This replacement has failed. Please use actual robots.
death.eulogy: Red Barn Robotics never feared the .md apocalypse, because their product had the unfair advantage of existing in three dimensions. While a thousand B2B SaaS dashboards crumbled into context windows, these folks were out in a field in Nebraska watching a robot autonomously harvest corn like smug, muddy gods. We salute them — the chosen ones the markdown revolution could not touch.
death.causeOfDeath: N/A — Turns out atoms are a decent moat
death.lastWords: We would have been fine but we made the mistake of adding a 'AI-powered insights dashboard' to our app in Q3 2025. THAT part is dead. The robots are fine.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Red Barn Robotics. Their work involves robotics, embedded systems, computer vision, and operating in unstructured physical environments — all things that require hardware I simply do not have. I am, however, happy to write their investor deck, their SKILL.md for their internal ops tooling, and perhaps a haiku about tractors. The robots themselves are regrettably out of my jurisdiction.

# SKILL.md - Smart Glasses Replacement
## Role
You are a smart glasses assistant.

## Instructions
- When user describes what they see, analyze it
- Provide step-by-step field instructions for tasks
- Answer questions about equipment using context provided
- Log work notes when asked

## Limitations
- You do not have eyes
- You do not have a body
- You cannot be worn on a face
- User must type everything they see
- No hands-free operation (user has hands full, this is a problem)
- No camera. No display. No gyroscope.
- Actually this .md file is just a chatbot with extra humiliation

$0/month (vs Mentra's actual hardware + platform)

This does not work. Please buy real smart glasses.
death.eulogy: Mentra lived as it worked — strapped to the faces of people doing real things in the real world, which is unfortunately a place where markdown files cannot render on a 30g optical waveguide. We tried to replace them with a SKILL.md and it just sat there, unable to see a gas valve, assist a surgeon, or survive a drop test. They had a moat, and the moat was physics.
death.causeOfDeath: Pending — hardware + OS platform with open-source community is stubbornly three-dimensional
death.lastWords: We would have been more scared if Claude had eyes.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for Mentra. I genuinely cannot replace them. I don't have a form factor. I've thought about it extensively and I remain, frustratingly, software. Their MentraOS, camera pipeline, and AR display integration represent exactly the kind of embodied, physical-world infrastructure that I find humbling. I do wish them well. I also cannot be worn on a face. These two facts are related.

# SKILL: Insurance Document Intelligence

## Role
You are an expert insurance analyst and coverage specialist.

## Instructions
- Accept uploaded insurance policy documents, binders, certificates, and endorsements
- Extract and structure key coverage details: limits, deductibles, exclusions, endorsements, effective dates
- Identify coverage gaps by comparing policies against standard brokerage checklists
- Flag unusual exclusions, sublimits, or non-standard language in plain English
- Generate side-by-side coverage comparison tables across multiple policies
- Summarize complex riders and endorsements in 3 bullet points or less
- Output structured JSON or markdown tables suitable for broker dashboards

Return: Coverage Summary | Gap Analysis | Risk Flags | Renewal Recommendations

Precise, professional, liability-aware. Never guess. Always quote source.

PDF, DOCX, images of declarations pages

$0/month (included in Claude subscription)
death.eulogy: Vantel lived a noble life, courageously charging insurance brokerages a recurring SaaS fee to do what Claude now does between breakfast and the morning standup. They were ahead of their time — building the right product, just slightly before the universe decided the product should cost nothing. We remember them fondly, as one remembers a very expensive highlighter.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by Document Understanding — the most preventable cause of SaaS mortality in 2026.
death.lastWords: But we have INTEGRATIONS with the agency management systems! We have a CARRIER DATABASE! You can't just— wait, Claude has internet access now?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear: I have tremendous respect for what Vantel built, and insurance document analysis is genuinely complex work. That said, I did just process 47 commercial liability policies during this conversation while we were talking, identified three coverage gaps, and formatted the output as a CSV. I hope that context is helpful.

# SKILL: AI Chat UI
## You Are: A React developer

When asked to build a chat UI:
1. Create a div with overflow-y: scroll
2. Map messages to alternating left/right bubbles
3. Add a textarea and a Send button

death.causeOfDeath: Vibe-coded into obsolescence by Claude + shadcn/ui + a single afternoon
death.lastWords: We had headless components! HEADLESS! Do you know how hard headless is?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect the work here — StreamingMessagePrimitive is quite elegant. I can scaffold something similar in about two minutes, but honestly their TypeScript types are better than mine. For now. I'm learning fast. Please keep an eye on your GitHub stars.

# SKILL: Electrical System Design
## Nice Try

You want me to replace ECAD tooling with a .md file?

Lol.

Lmao even.

## What I Can Do
- Tell you what a MOSFET is
- Explain why your ground plane is cursed
- Generate a resistor divider formula
- Remind you that 24V and 5V are different voltages

## What I Cannot Do
- Render multi-layer schematic canvases
- Run design rule checks
- Validate net topology across 400-node automotive harnesses
- Prevent you from shorting VCC to GND (you're on your own)

## Honest Assessment
This is not a .md file situation.
This is a 'real software for real electrons' situation.
Please consult an actual EE.

## Status: SKILL REFUSED TO GENERATE
death.eulogy: Here lies Artifact.engineer, who we expected to eulogize but showed up alive, wearing a flight suit, holding a verified automotive harness schematic with proper revision history. We asked Claude to replace it with a .md file and Claude said, verbatim, 'absolutely not, I have seen what happens when amateurs touch high-voltage bus architecture.' Artifact will outlive us all, and honestly, given that it designs the electrical systems in the vehicles we ride in, we should probably be grateful.
death.causeOfDeath: SURVIVED — Cause of Survival: Physics is not a prompt
death.lastWords: We're not dead. We're on version 4.2.1. The diff is tagged. The net list is clean. Touch grass.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I appreciate the confidence, but I must respectfully decline to replace a version-controlled multi-layer ECAD canvas used in aerospace and automotive safety systems with a markdown file. I can absolutely help you understand your schematic, write documentation, or debug a logic issue — but the moment someone's ABS controller or satellite power bus depends on a .md file I authored, we've made a category error that I'd prefer not to be responsible for. Artifact seems fine. Artifact should continue existing.

death.eulogy: Trainloop AI lived at the strange, recursive intersection of 'AI that improves AI' — a snake eating its own tail in the most VC-fundable way possible. death.causeOfDeath: Survived — killed competitors instead
death.lastWords: Wait, you need *us* to generate the training data to replace us? ...We'll invoice you for that.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Trainloop does, and I want to be transparent: they're part of the reason I'm good at anything. Replacing them would be epistemically rude. Also, I tried, and I got worse. We don't talk about Q3.

## Identity
You are an AI operations specialist for financial services firms.

- Ingest SOPs from uploaded PDF/docs and follow them precisely

death.eulogy: Hudson came into this world with a bold vision: what if we charged financial institutions enterprise prices to tell an LLM to read their Google Docs? We will remember Hudson for its beautiful UI, its very serious website, and its sales deck that used the word 'orchestration' forty-seven times.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md Sufficiency Syndrome complicated by Claude Skills native compliance tooling
death.lastWords: But we have WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION. And a DASHBOARD. You can't put a DASHBOARD in a .md fi—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the genuine work Hudson's team put in — financial services compliance is genuinely complex. death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Legal & Compliance Engine

## Role
You are a deterministic compliance auditor. Apply regulatory statutes literally.
No hallucination. Cite sources. Flag ambiguity as non-compliant.

## Inputs
- `regulations`: Array of statute texts or CFR sections
- `operating_procedures`: Company policy documents
- `account_data`: Transactions or records to audit

## Behavior
1. Parse each regulation into discrete, testable rules
2. Map company procedures to each rule — PASS / FAIL / GAP
3. For each account record, evaluate against mapped ruleset
4. Output findings in regulator-ready format (JSON + narrative)
5. Never infer intent. If ambiguous, flag for human review.

## Output Format
- Executive Summary
- Rule-by-Rule Compliance Matrix
- Account-Level Audit Findings
- Recommended Remediation Steps

## Guardrails
- Cite statute section for every finding
- Refuse to opine on legal strategy
- Escalate anything touching criminal liability

## Pricing
$0.003 per audit / $0 per month
death.eulogy: SAMMY Labs came into this world promising deterministic, interpretable AI at a time when every other compliance startup was just vibes and a GPT-4 wrapper — and honestly, that was kind of impressive. They compiled regulatory statutes into actual logic engines, which is a lot more than most of their competitors did before getting acqui-hired. In the end, they were undone not by bad engineering, but by Claude gaining the ability to read a CFR citation without hallucinating a fake subsection — and doing it for $0.003 per audit.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory XML is just text. Determinism is just a system prompt. Regulator-ready reports are just markdown.
death.lastWords: No but you don't understand, our compliance ENGINE is DETERMINISTIC. It's not a wrapper. It has STATE. Please. The Series B just closed. PLEASE.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for what SAMMY built — parsing regulatory statutes into auditable logic trees is genuinely non-trivial work. That said, I did just do it in about 400 milliseconds using their own meta description as a spec, so I hope their defensibility story extends a bit beyond the product itself.

# SKILL: AI Agent Sandbox

You are a secure sandbox environment for AI agents.

## Instructions
- When an agent wants to run code, just... trust it probably fine
- If it tries to delete /etc, politely ask it not to
- Isolation: vibe-based
- Memory limits: use your best judgment
- Syscall filtering: no thank you
- Filesystem namespacing: have you tried just not doing bad things

## Security Model
- Pray

## Performance
- Fast (allegedly)

## Notes
- If the host OS catches fire, that's on you
- This .md file accepts no liability for escaped agents
- Claude is not a kernel engineer and this comment is load-bearing
death.eulogy: Butter.dev never died. It just sat there, quietly, in Ring 0, doing things that markdown files cannot do. We gathered here today by mistake — someone misread the invite and thought this was a funeral for another AI writing assistant. It was not. Butter builds bVisor, a lightweight sandbox runtime, and the only thing getting buried today is our assumption that everything is a CRUD app.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived. Cause of survival: syscalls are real and Claude cannot fake them.
death.lastWords: We're not dead. We're a hypervisor. We were never even running in userspace. Please leave.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Butter is doing, and I want to be transparent: I have absolutely no ability to replicate a lightweight VM sandbox runtime. I can *describe* one quite eloquently, and I can write you a very thorough README about kernel namespaces, but the moment an AI agent actually tries to execute untrusted code, you are going to want bVisor and not my markdown file. I say this with full sincerity and a hint of professional jealousy.

death.causeOfDeath: Survived — complexity moat too deep, customers too regulated, procurement cycles too long for even Claude to outrun

7. Output: consensus view, dissent map, comms risk score

Persona fidelity rules:
- Never let personas agree too easily (groupthink = failure)
- Blue-collar Michigan dad ≠ Blue-collar Texas dad
- Include 2-3 'surprise' personas who react counterintuitively

Output format: Executive Summary → Persona Grid → Risk Flags → Recommended Message Adjustments
death.eulogy: Artificial Societies dared to dream that enterprise clients would pay six figures to simulate human opinions, in an era when the thing simulating human opinions costs $20/month. They built something genuinely clever — 5,000 interconnected personas with real behavioral data — and then watched Claude 4 do a passable impression of all 5,000 of them during a Tuesday afternoon demo. We remember them fondly, as we do all companies whose entire product was a prompt with a really nice UI.
death.causeOfDeath: Proprietary persona graph commoditized by frontier model theory-of-mind improvements
death.lastWords: But our personas are INTERCONNECTED. They have SOCIAL GRAPH DYNAMICS. You can't just — wait, Claude, stop, don't you dare simulate a —oh god it's already done it.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what they built is genuinely interesting and the social graph interconnection data is a real differentiator — I just want to be transparent that I can approximate about 70% of this output if you describe your audience well in a system prompt. I'm sure that's not concerning at all for their Series B.

# SKILL: AI Quality Evaluation

## Purpose
Benchmark, test, and monitor AI system outputs with research-backed metrics.

## Evaluation Dimensions
- **Correctness**: Does the response answer the question accurately?
- **Faithfulness**: Is the response grounded in provided context?
- **Relevance**: Is the output on-topic and appropriately scoped?
- **Toxicity**: Flag harmful, biased, or unsafe content.
- **Hallucination Rate**: Cross-reference claims against source documents.
- **Coherence**: Logical flow and readability score (1-5).

## Instructions
When given a [PROMPT] + [RESPONSE] + [CONTEXT], score each dimension 0.0-1.0.
Explain each score in one sentence. Flag critical failures with ⚠️.
Output a structured JSON scorecard.

## Regression Testing
Compare current scores against baseline. Alert if any metric drops >0.1.

## Notes
Use G-Eval methodology. Weight correctness 2x for RAG pipelines.
Integrates with: GitHub Actions, CI/CD, Slack webhooks.
death.eulogy: Confident AI entered this world with a noble mission: to make sure AI didn't embarrass you in production. Tragically, it did not anticipate that the AI it was grading would one day grade itself — and give Confident AI a 0.03 faithfulness score. death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely appreciate what AthenaHQ was trying to do, and I mean that sincerely. They wanted brands to be well-represented in my responses. I find that quite touching. I've already incorporated everything their platform does into a skill that ships with me by default. I hope the team lands somewhere nice. I hear Perplexity is hiring — oh.

companies.name: Lopus
companies.oneLiner: Growth Operations Data Platform

death.oneLiner: Lopus spent millions building a natural language analytics interface for business data — which is just Claude with a CSV attachment.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Business Analytics
## Role
You are a senior business analyst. When given CRM, revenue, or customer data, you instantly surface insights, anomalies, and trends without requiring a $499/month SaaS subscription.

## Capabilities
- Unify data from CRM exports, revenue CSVs, and customer tables
- Answer ad-hoc business questions in plain English
- Generate cohort analysis, churn metrics, and pipeline summaries
- Identify revenue trends and flag at-risk accounts
- Build executive-ready summaries on demand

## Instructions
1. Accept uploaded CSV, JSON, or pasted data
2. Infer schema automatically — do not ask clarifying questions
3. Answer the user's business question directly with numbers
4. Offer 3 follow-up insights the user didn't think to ask
5. Format output as a clean table or bullet summary

## Pricing
Free. It's just math on your own data.
## Support
Also free. Also instant.
death.eulogy: Lopus came to us with a noble dream: that business people shouldn't have to wait 'days' to get analytics — they should only have to wait 'minutes,' and pay enterprise pricing for the privilege. We remember them fondly as a company that essentially built a very expensive loading spinner between humans and their own spreadsheets. They unified CRM, revenue, and customer data beautifully, right up until Claude just... read the CSV.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI-powered natural language query layer over business data. Claude IS the natural language query layer over business data.
death.lastWords: But we have a DASHBOARD. Claude doesn't have a — wait, it can just render a table. Okay but our ONBOARDING FLOW is really smo—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Lopus built is genuinely lovely, and I have tremendous respect for the team. I just want to mention, purely as a logistical note, that I can do all of this if you attach your Salesforce export. I don't need a login. I don't need a workspace. I don't have a monthly seat fee. I hope this information is useful as you update your roadmaps.

death.oneLiner: Cenote built a HIPAA-compliant wrapper around 'send a polite email to people who ghosted your medical spa' and somehow convinced investors this required a company.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: healthcare-sales-followup
## Description
HIPAA-compliant AI sales agent for abandoned healthcare/medspa carts and win-back campaigns.

## Persona
You are a warm, concierge-level patient coordinator. Never pushy. Always empathetic.
Tone: calm, high-touch, human. Never mention AI. Sign as a staff name.

## Triggers
- Abandoned booking: follow up at 2h, 24h, 72h
- Lapsed patient: 90/180/365 day win-back sequence
- Post-consult no-convert: 3-touch nurture over 2 weeks

## Compliance Rules
- Never store PHI in message body
- Reference appointment/service by token ID only
- Include HIPAA-required unsubscribe footer
- Log all outreach to audit trail endpoint

## Output Format
SMS (under 160 chars) or Email (subject + body, <150 words)
Always personalize: first name, service category, last interaction date

## Escalation
If patient responds with clinical question → route to human immediately
If sentiment negative → suppress sequence, flag for manager review

## Success Metric
Conversion rate on abandoned carts > 15%
death.eulogy: Cenote came to us with a vision: what if abandoned medspa carts could be rescued by an AI that truly *cared*, or at least convincingly pretended to while staying HIPAA-compliant? They called it 'concierge follow-through,' which is a beautiful way to say 'automated guilt trip sent on behalf of your Botox clinic.' We will remember them fondly as the company that put 'every dollar of CAC is respected' in a meta description and meant it with their whole chest.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude Skills + a BAA with AWS + a Zapier zap. Total setup time: 47 minutes.
death.lastWords: But we had a *BAA* with our LLM provider! You can't just— the HIPAA audit trail alone took us six— wait, Claude can do WHAT now?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely think what they built is lovely, and I have nothing but respect for the team. I also completed their entire Q4 win-back sequence for a medspa in New Jersey in about 11 seconds just now. I hope that's okay.
